from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a sports team into a fluent, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Templates for known predicates
    _templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "{subject} has {object} members",
        "season": "{subject} played in the {object} season",
        "league": "{subject} competes in the {object}",
        "manager": "{subject} is managed by {object}",
        "ground": "{subject} plays at {object}",
        "fullName": "{subject}'s full name is {object}",
        "chairman": "{subject}'s chairman is {object}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {object}",
        "nickname": "{subject} is nicknamed {object}",
        "chairmanTitle": "{subject}'s {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "part": "{subject} includes {object}",
        "mayor": "{subject}'s mayor is {object}",
        "champions": "{subject} champions are {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {object}",
        "club": "{subject} plays for {object}",
        "capital": "{subject}'s capital is {object}",
        "currency": "{subject} uses the {object}",
        "demonym": "People from {subject} are called {object}",
        "language": "{subject} language is {object}",
        "leaderParty": "{subject} leader belongs to {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subject} official language is {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "youthclub": "{subject} youth club is {object}",
        "operator": "{subject} is operated by {object}",
        "tenant": "{subject} is tenant of {object}",
        "city": "{subject} is in the city of {object}",
        "state": "{subject} is in the state of {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject many times
    from collections import OrderedDict
    subject_map: OrderedDict[str, list[Triple]] = OrderedDict()
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    subject_sentences = []
    for subject, sub_triples in subject_map.items():
        phrases = []
        for i, t in enumerate(sub_triples):
            tmpl = _templates.get(t.predicate, "{subject} {predicate} {object}")
            phrase = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, predicate=t.predicate, object=t.object)
            # Omit the subject in subsequent phrases for the same entity
            if i > 0:
                # Remove leading subject and possible possessive "'s "
                if phrase.startswith(f"{subject}'s "):
                    phrase = phrase[len(f"{subject}'s "):]
                elif phrase.startswith(subject + " "):
                    phrase = phrase[len(subject) + 1 :]
            phrases.append(phrase.rstrip())

        # Combine phrases for this subject
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            subj_sentence = phrases[0]
        else:
            subj_sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
        subject_sentences.append(subj_sentence)

    # Join all subject sentences into one final sentence
    # Join subject sentences with proper punctuation to form a single coherent sentence
    final_sentence = ". ".join(s.rstrip('.') for s in subject_sentences) + "."
    return final_sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
